Assistant.text: I'll explore the codebase to give you an overview. Let me start with the key entry points and docs.

ToolResults.content:
1
2 3 🍞 Ciabatta 4 5
6 7 **Artifact publishing made easy.** 8 9 Ciabatta is a fast, cross-platform CLI for publishing and pulling build 10 artifacts to and from common registries — Nexus (raw, **npm**, and **PyPI**), S3, 11 Artifactory, Docker, and ECR — driven by a single declarative TOML file. It picks 12 up branch / commit / tag / build-number metadata from whatever CI system you run 13 on, runs multiple publish jobs in parallel, and shows progress in a friendly 14 terminal UI. 15 16 ``` 17 _____ _ _ _ _ 18 / ____(_) | | | | | | 19 | | _ __ _| |__ __ _| |_| |_ __ _ 20 | | | |/ _` | '_ \ / _` | __| __/ _` | 21 | |____| | (_| | |_) | (_| | |_| || (_| | 22 \_____|_|\__,_|_.__/ \__,_|\__|\__\__,_| 23 ``` 24 25 ## Why Ciabatta 26 27 - **One config, many registries.** Describe your registries and publish 28 "recipes" once in `.ciabatta/ciabatta.toml`; run any combination of them with 29 a single command. 30 - **CI-aware.** Automatically resolves `CIABATTA_BRANCH`, `CIABATTA_COMMIT`, 31 `CIABATTA_TAG`, and `CIABATTA_BUILD_NUMBER` from GitLab, GitHub Actions, 32 Jenkins, CircleCI, Azure DevOps, or Bitbucket — and lets you template them 33 into publish paths. 34 - **Parallel with live progress.** Run several recipes at once and watch each 35 one in a `ratatui`-powered TUI (or `--no-tui` for plain CI logs). 36 - **Push *and* pull.** Because Ciabatta knows where things live, it can fetch 37 artifacts back down, not just upload them. 38 - **Bring your own auth.** Login is handled by your own scripts — Ciabatta just 39 makes the resolved variables available to them as environment variables. 40 - **Truly drop-in.** Linux builds are statically linked (musl), so there is no 41 glibc version requirement: download, extract, run, on any distro. 42 43 ## Installation 44 45 ### From crates.io 46 47 ```bash 48 cargo install ciabatta 49 ``` 50 51 ### Pre-built binaries 52 53 Download the archive for your platform from the 54 [latest release](https://github.com/forsyth-creations/ciabatta/releases/latest). 55 Each archive includes an install script that puts the binary on your `PATH`. 56 57 **Linux / macOS** 58 ```bash 59 tar xzf ciabatta-linux-x86_64.tar.gz 60 ./install.sh # installs to /usr/local/bin (uses sudo if needed) 61 ./install.sh ~/.local/bin # or pick your own directory 62 ``` 63 64 **Windows** (PowerShell) 65 ```powershell 66 Expand-Archive ciabatta-windows-x86_64.zip 67 .\install.ps1 # installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\ciabatta and adds it to your PATH 68 .\install.ps1 -InstallDir C:\tools\ciabatta # or pick your own directory 69 ``` 70 71 Builds are published for Linux (x86_64 / aarch64, static), macOS 72 (x86_64 / aarch64), and Windows (x86_64). 73 74 ## Quick start 75 76 ```bash 77 # 1. Scaffold a .ciabatta/ directory with a starter config 78 ciabatta init --ci github 79 80 # 2. See what recipes are available 81 ciabatta list 82 83 # 3. Dry-run to see exactly what would happen 84 ciabatta push release_frontend --dry-run 85 86 # 4. Publish for real (pushes multiple recipes in parallel) 87 ciabatta push release_frontend release_backend 88 89 # 5. Pull an artifact back down 90 ciabatta pull release_frontend 91 ``` 92 93 Ciabatta discovers your project by walking up to find the `.ciabatta/` 94 directory; the directory **above** it is treated as the project root that 95 artifacts are published from. 96 97 ## Commands 98 99 | Command | What it does | 100 | --- | --- | 101 | `ciabatta push [RECIPE...]` | Push one or more recipes in parallel (all if none named). | 102 | `ciabatta pull [RECIPE...]` | Download artifacts for one or more recipes. | 103 | `ciabatta deploy [RECIPE...]` | Run a deploy: a DAG of dependent script steps with error-recovery branches. `--gui` for a live browser view, `--build` for a visual flowchart editor. | 104 | `ciabatta list` | List all recipes defined in the config. | 105 | `ciabatta init [--ci SYSTEM]` | Create a `.ciabatta/` directory with a starter `ciabatta.toml`. | 106 | `ciabatta configure` | Interactively add a registry (and optionally a recipe) — no hand-editing TOML. | 107 | `ciabatta configure auto` | Analyze the project and pick recipes from an interactive checklist (Docker → ECR/Nexus, Rust binaries → crates.io / S3 / Nexus). | 108 | `ciabatta tui` (alias `browse`) | Interactive browser — inspect registries, check paths, push on demand. | 109 | `ciabatta analyze` | Build the project's dependency graph and serve an interactive view. | 110 | `ciabatta watch ` | Run a command and stream its logs into a live, searchable web view with bookmarks and notification triggers. | 111 | `ciabatta config show` | Print the resolved configuration. | 112 | `ciabatta config reference` | Show documentation on the config format and options. | 113 114 Useful flags on `push` / `pull`: 115 116 - `-e, --env KEY=VALUE` — set a variable. **Command-line values always override 117 CI-derived ones.** Repeatable. 118 - `--dry-run` — show what would happen without publishing or fetching. 119 - `--no-tui` — disable the TUI and stream plain progress to stdout (ideal for CI). 120 - `-c, --config PATH` — use a specific config file instead of discovery. 121 122 Global flags (any command): 123 124 - `--debug` — enable debug logging to stderr. You can also set `CIABATTA_DEBUG=1`, 125 or `CIABATTA_LOG=ciabatta=trace` for finer control. 126 127 When a recipe's `local_artifact_path` is a **directory**, Ciabatta uploads each 128 file in it individually, recreating the folder structure under the recipe's 129 `publish_path` (creating sub-folders in the registry as needed) — so 130 `local_artifact_path = "frontend/dist"` publishes the whole `dist` tree. 131 132 In the `ciabatta tui` browser, press `e` on a registry to **explore** its remote 133 contents — navigate folders and see which artifacts already exist, which is handy 134 when deciding on a `publish_path`. 135 136 ## Configuration 137 138 Ciabatta reads `.ciabatta/ciabatta.toml`. Registries describe *where* things go; 139 recipes describe *what* to publish and *how*. 140 141 The fastest way to start is `ciabatta configure` (add a registry interactively) 142 or `ciabatta configure auto` (let Ciabatta inspect the repo and suggest recipes). 143 You can also edit the file by hand: 144 145 ```toml 146 [system] 147 ci = "github" # gitlab | github | jenkins | circleci | azure | bitbucket 148 containers = "docker" # docker | podman — when omitted, Ciabatta auto-detects what 149 # is installed (prefers podman, then docker; asks you to 150 # choose if both are present). 151 152 [registries.nexus] 153 # url and login_script expand environment variables with bash-style defaults, 154 # so one config can target different environments. 155 type = "nexus" 156 url = "https://${NEXUS_HOST:-nexus.example.com}" # bare Nexus host 157 repository = "raw-hosted" # which repo artifacts publish into 158 format = "raw" # raw | npm | pypi 159 tls_verify = true 160 needs_auth = true 161 162 [registries.s3] 163 type = "s3" 164 url = "s3://my-artifacts-bucket" # the bucket, with the s3:// scheme 165 166 # A simple recipe: copy a local artifact to a templated publish path. 167 [recipies.release_frontend] 168 registry = "nexus" 169 local_artifact_path = "frontend/dist" 170 publish_path = "frontend/{CIABATTA_BRANCH}/{CIABATTA_COMMIT}/frontend" 171 172 # A scripted recipe: run your own push/pull scripts with the variables injected. 173 [recipies.release_backend.push] 174 bash_script = "scripts/release_backend.sh" 175 176 [recipies.release_backend.pull] 177 bash_script = "scripts/pull_backend.sh" 178 ``` 179 180 A few rules worth knowing: 181 182 - If a `publish_path` references a variable that isn't set, Ciabatta **errors 183 immediately** rather than publishing to a half-resolved path. 184 - Stage commands, login scripts, and bash recipes all receive every resolved 185 `CIABATTA_*` variable (plus anything you pass with `-e`) in their environment. 186 187 Run `ciabatta config reference` for the full, always-up-to-date field listing. 188 189 ### Nexus repositories: raw, npm, and PyPI 190 191 A Nexus registry picks its target repository and publish mechanism with three 192 fields: 193 194 - `repository` — the Nexus repo name (e.g. `raw-hosted`, `npm-hosted`). When set, 195 `url` is the bare Nexus host and `/repository/` is appended for you. 196 When omitted, `url` is used as the full repository URL (backwards compatible). 197 - `format` — `raw` (default), `npm`, or `pypi`, selecting how the push happens. 198 - `base_path` — *raw only*: an optional prefix prepended to every recipe's 199 `publish_path`, so raw artifacts land under a common folder. 200 201 | `format` | How a push works | Requirements | 202 | --- | --- | --- | 203 | `raw` | HTTP `PUT` (pull is HTTP `GET`) | none | 204 | `npm` | `npm publish --registry ` | `npm` on `PATH` | 205 | `pypi` | `twine upload --repository-url ` | `twine` on `PATH` | 206 207 For `npm` / `pypi` recipes, `local_artifact_path` is the package tarball or the 208 `dist/` directory to publish, and `publish_path` is not used (the package name and 209 version determine where it lands). Both read credentials from 210 `CIABATTA__USER` / `_PASS`; npm also accepts a `CIABATTA__TOKEN` 211 bearer token. `ciabatta pull` supports only `raw` repositories — pull npm/PyPI 212 packages with their native clients. 213 214 ```toml 215 # Publish an npm package straight to a Nexus npm repository. 216 [registries.sdk] 217 type = "nexus" 218 url = "https://nexus.example.com" 219 repository = "npm-hosted" 220 format = "npm" 221 222 [recipies.sdk] 223 registry = "sdk" 224 local_artifact_path = "packages/sdk" # tarball or package directory 225 ``` 226 227 ### S3 228 229 An S3 registry drives the AWS CLI, so it's just a bucket URL: set `url` to 230 `s3://` and each recipe's `publish_path` becomes the object key. 231 232 ```toml 233 [registries.s3] 234 type = "s3" # inferred when the name contains "s3" 235 url = "s3://my-artifacts-bucket" 236 237 [recipies.release] 238 registry = "s3" 239 local_artifact_path = "target/release/app" 240 publish_path = "app/{CIABATTA_BRANCH}/{CIABATTA_COMMIT}/app" 241 # uploads to s3://my-artifacts-bucket/app///app 242 ``` 243 244 - **Auth** uses the standard AWS credential chain — `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` / 245 `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `AWS_PROFILE`, or an instance/role profile — so no 246 `login_script` is needed. Set `AWS_REGION` if your bucket isn't in the CLI's 247 default region. 248 - The `aws` CLI must be installed and configured on the machine or CI runner; 249 Ciabatta shells out to `aws s3 cp` for both push and pull. 250 251 ### Docker / ECR images 252 253 For `docker`- and `ecr`-type registries, point a recipe at a **locally-built 254 image** with `local_image`. Ciabatta retags it to the registry's target 255 reference and pushes it — so you don't have to bake the registry URL into your 256 `docker build`: 257 258 ```toml 259 [registries.myecr] 260 type = "ecr" # inferred when the name contains "ecr" 261 url = "123456789.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com" 262 263 [recipies.app] 264 registry = "myecr" 265 local_image = "app:latest" # a local image (name or name:tag) 266 publish_path = "app:{CIABATTA_COMMIT}" # remote image ref (repo[:tag]) 267 268 # Build the image locally; ciabatta handles the tag + push. 269 [recipies.app.push] 270 pre = "docker build -t app:latest ." 271 ``` 272 273 On push this runs `docker tag app:latest /app:` then 274 `docker push /app:`. On pull it pulls that remote reference and 275 retags it back to `app:latest`. Omit `publish_path` to reuse `local_image` 276 verbatim as the remote reference. ECR auto-logs in via 277 `aws ecr get-login-password`; plain Docker registries use 278 `CIABATTA__USER` / `_PASS`. 279 280 ## Stages 281 282 Every direction runs as a four-stage state machine, and the TUI shows progress 283 through each stage live: 284 285 ``` 286 Push: login → pre-push → push → post-push 287 Pull: login → pre-pull → pull → post-pull 288 Deploy: login → pre-deploy → deploy → post-deploy 289 ``` 290 291 Override any stage with an arbitrary command — bash, python, a compiled binary, 292 anything runnable. Unset stages fall back to their defaults (login authenticates, 293 `pre`/`post` do nothing, `main` runs the built-in registry action). Each command 294 runs via `sh -c` from the project root with all `CIABATTA_*` vars in its 295 environment. 296 297 ```toml 298 [recipies.frontend] 299 registry = "nexus" 300 local_artifact_path = "frontend/dist" 301 publish_path = "front/{CIABATTA_COMMIT}/dist" 302 303 # Overrides for the push direction only: 304 [recipies.frontend.push] 305 pre = "python scripts/bundle.py" # pre-push 306 post = "./scripts/notify.sh deployed" # post-push 307 # login + push (main) use their defaults 308 309 [recipies.frontend.pull] 310 post = "echo pulled $CIABATTA_COMMIT" 311 ``` 312 313 | Stage | Override key | Default | 314 | --- | --- | --- | 315 | login | `login` | registry `login_script`, or `CIABATTA__USER`/`_PASS` credentials | 316 | pre-push / pre-pull | `pre` | nothing | 317 | push / pull | `main` | built-in registry action (or legacy `bash_script`) | 318 | post-push / post-pull | `post` | nothing | 319 320 ## Deploys 321 322 A **deploy** is a third recipe direction, run with `ciabatta deploy`. Instead of 323 a single registry transfer, its `deploy` phase runs a **DAG of dependent script 324 steps** — build → migrate → release, and so on. Deploys are "just another 325 recipe": they live in `[recipies..deploy]`, show up in `ciabatta list`, 326 and work with menus (`--cookbook`). 327 328 The **step graph lives in its own flowchart file**, referenced from your config, 329 so a complex pipeline doesn't clutter `ciabatta.toml`: 330 331 ```toml 332 # .ciabatta/ciabatta.toml 333 [recipies.web.deploy] 334 flowchart = ".ciabatta/deploys.toml" # the step DAG (a separate file) 335 pre = "scripts/notify_start.sh" # optional login/pre/post phase hooks 336 ``` 337 338 ```toml 339 # .ciabatta/deploys.toml — each top-level entry is a series of dependent steps. 340 [web] 341 REQUIRED_ENV = ["DEPLOY_TOKEN", "AWS_REGION"] # gate the whole flowchart 342 343 [[web.steps]] 344 name = "build" 345 script = "scripts/build.sh" # a bash file… (or use run = "…" inline) 346 347 [[web.steps]] 348 name = "migrate" 349 script = "scripts/migrate.sh" 350 needs = ["build"] # runs once "build" succeeds (a DAG edge) 351 on_error = "fix_migrate" # on failure, jump to a recovery node 352 353 [[web.steps]] # a recovery node: a choice of fixes 354 name = "fix_migrate" 355 recover = true 356 message = "Migration failed — choose how to recover:" 357 retry = "migrate" # re-run this step after a fix succeeds 358 options = [ 359 { label = "Roll back", script = "scripts/rollback.sh" }, 360 { label = "Force unlock", run = "make unlock", default = true }, 361 ] 362 363 [[web.steps]] 364 name = "release" 365 script = "scripts/release.sh" 366 needs = ["migrate"] 367 ``` 368 369 Steps whose `needs` are all satisfied become eligible to run; the graph is 370 validated up front (missing edges, non-recovery `on_error` targets, and cycles 371 are rejected before anything runs). 372 373 **`REQUIRED_ENV`** lists variables the flowchart needs. Before any phase runs, 374 each is checked; if one is empty or unset the deploy is aborted — the missing 375 names are printed to the console and shown in the `--gui` view, and no step runs. 376 (You can also set `REQUIRED_ENV` on the `[recipies..deploy]` table; the two 377 lists are merged.) 378 379 ### Build variables are auto-sourced 380 381 Every `ciabatta deploy` **auto-sources the `CIABATTA_*` build variables from your 382 local git** (`CIABATTA_BRANCH` / `_COMMIT` / `_TAG` / `_BUILD_NUMBER`, plus the 383 derived `CIABATTA_PATH`) and makes them available to every step, `run` command, 384 and phase hook — the same set `ciabatta source` prints, so you don't need to 385 `eval "$(ciabatta source)"` first. This happens regardless of `--local` / 386 `CIABATTA_ENV`, so a deploy script can reference `$CIABATTA_COMMIT` on a plain 387 dev-machine run: 388 389 ```toml 390 [[web.steps]] 391 name = "release" 392 run = "./scripts/release.sh --tag $CIABATTA_COMMIT" 393 ``` 394 395 Only *unset* variables are filled in: anything you provide explicitly — from a CI 396 system, the ambient environment, or `-e CIABATTA_BRANCH=…` — takes precedence. A 397 non-git directory is fine; the deploy just runs without the git-derived values. 398 399 ### Error recovery ("if error") 400 401 When a step with `on_error` fails, control jumps to its **recovery node**, which 402 presents a list of fix `options`: 403 404 - With **`--gui`**, the browser shows fix-it buttons — click one to run that fix. 405 - Without a UI (plain / CI), the option marked **`default = true`** runs 406 automatically (unattended self-heal); if none is marked, the deploy fails and 407 prints the available remedies. 408 - After a fix succeeds, **`retry`** re-runs the named step. Retry loops are 409 bounded so a persistently failing step can't spin forever. 410 411 ### Watching and building deploys in the browser 412 413 ```bash 414 ciabatta deploy web --gui # live view: flowchart + streaming logs + fix buttons 415 ciabatta deploy --build # visual flowchart editor → copy the generated TOML 416 ``` 417 418 `--gui` serves a local page (default `--port 8088`) that shows each step lighting 419 up as it runs, per-step logs, and interactive recovery. `--build` opens a visual 420 builder that needs no project: lay out steps, edges, and recovery options, then 421 copy the emitted TOML into your flowchart file. Already have a pipeline? Paste 422 its TOML into the builder's import box to keep editing it visually. 423 424 | Phase | Override key | Default | 425 | --- | --- | --- | 426 | login | `login` | nothing (deploys usually authenticate inside a step) | 427 | pre-deploy | `pre` | nothing | 428 | deploy | the `steps` DAG | runs the flowchart | 429 | post-deploy | `post` | nothing | 430 431 ## Credentials 432 433 When a registry has **no** `login_script` and no `login` override, Ciabatta reads 434 per-registry credentials from the environment: 435 436 ``` 437 CIABATTA__USER CIABATTA__PASS 438 ``` 439 440 `` is the registry's section name, uppercased — so `[registries.nexus]` 441 uses `CIABATTA_NEXUS_USER` / `CIABATTA_NEXUS_PASS`. They're applied per type: 442 443 - **Nexus (raw) / Artifactory** — sent as HTTP basic auth on the upload/download. 444 - **Nexus (npm)** — written to a throwaway npmrc for `npm publish`; prefers a 445 `CIABATTA__TOKEN`, otherwise basic auth from `_USER` / `_PASS`. 446 - **Nexus (PyPI)** — passed to `twine upload` as `-u` / `-p`. 447 - **Docker** — `docker login -u $USER --password-stdin`. 448 - **ECR** — auto-login via `aws ecr get-login-password` (no credentials needed). 449 - **S3** — uses the standard AWS credential chain (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, …). 450 451 ## Analyze 452 453 `ciabatta analyze` maps how your repository is wired together and serves an 454 interactive dependency graph laid out in columns: 455 456 ``` 457 [requirements] → dependencies → internal packages → publish points 458 (optional) (crates.io, (your crates, (crates.io, plus 459 npm, pip, npm/python packages, ciabatta-managed 460 dockerhub) workspaces, modules) registries) 461 ``` 462 463 It scans `Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `requirements.txt` / `pyproject.toml`, 464 `Dockerfile`s, and `.gitlab-ci.yml` (its `image:` / `services:` container 465 images) for external dependencies, identifies the internal packages in the repo, 466 and derives publish points from your ciabatta recipes (and a publishable crate → 467 crates.io). The result is written as JSON and served at `http://127.0.0.1:8080`, 468 where you can click any node for details. 469 470 **Accurate Rust versions.** A `Cargo.toml` dependency is only a *requirement* 471 (`serde = "1"`), so `analyze` reads the workspace `Cargo.lock` and shows the 472 concrete version cargo actually locked (`serde 1.0.228`), keeping the original 473 requirement alongside it. The lockfile is also how it tells your own crates 474 apart from crates.io: a workspace member or a `path`/`git` dependency is 475 classified as **internal** rather than being drawn as an external crates.io box, 476 so internal-crate edges no longer masquerade as third-party dependencies. 477 478 **Structure tab.** The view has a **Structure** tab showing the repository's 479 folder tree, pruned to just the folders that contain a dependency manifest 480 (`Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, …). Click a folder — or a 481 manifest within it — to see the package it defines, its resolved dependencies 482 (internal and external), and where it publishes; every entry links back into the 483 graph. 484 485 **Publish scripts.** Developers often publish from shell scripts, so `analyze` 486 also reads `.sh` files (anywhere in the tree, plus `.ciabatta/` and any script 487 referenced by your config) and turns registry-push commands into publish points: 488 `docker`/`podman push`, `aws s3 cp`/`sync`, `cargo publish`, `npm`/`yarn 489 publish`, `twine upload`, `helm push`, and `curl` uploads (`-T` / `--upload-file` 490 / `PUT`). Each is wired to the package that owns the script, and — unlike a 491 ciabatta recipe — is **not** flagged as ciabatta-managed. 492 493 ```bash 494 ciabatta analyze # write JSON + open the live view on :8080 495 ciabatta analyze --port 9000 # use a different port … [truncated: 24000 of 31062 bytes shown]

1 [package]
    2 name = "ciabatta"
    3 version = "0.1.17"
    4 edition = "2024"
    5 description = "A command line tool for publishing artifacts to various registries"
    6 license = "MIT"
    7 readme = "README.md"
    8 repository = "https://github.com/forsyth-creations/ciabatta"
    9 homepage = "https://forsyth-creations.github.io/Ciabatta/"
   10 keywords = ["artifact", "publishing", "cli", "registry", "ci"]
   11 categories = ["command-line-utilities", "development-tools"]
   12 
   13 [[bin]]
   14 name = "ciabatta"
   15 path = "src/main.rs"
   16 
   17 [dependencies]
   18 clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive", "env"] }
   19 ratatui = "0.29"
   20 crossterm = { version = "0.28", features = ["event-stream"] }
   21 tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
   22 serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
   23 toml = "0.8"
   24 anyhow = "1"
   25 tracing = "0.1"
   26 tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
   27 reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["json", "rustls-tls", "charset", "http2"] }
   28 futures = "0.3"
   29 regex = "1"
   30 glob = "0.3"
   31 chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
   32 serde_json = "1"
   33 rustfmt = "0.10.0"

Assistant.text: This is a substantial project. Let me look at the module structure to understand the main areas before summarizing.

ToolResults.content:
1 use clap::{Parser, Subcommand};
    2 
    3 #[derive(Parser, Debug)]
    4 #[command(name = "ciabatta", about = "Artifact publishing made easy 🍞", version)]
    5 pub struct Cli {
    6     /// Enable debug logging to stderr. Can also be enabled by setting the
    7     /// CIABATTA_DEBUG environment variable (to any non-empty value other than
    8     /// "0"/"false"). For finer control, set CIABATTA_LOG (e.g. `ciabatta=trace`).
    9     #[arg(long, global = true)]
   10     pub debug: bool,
   11 
   12     #[command(subcommand)]
   13     pub command: Commands,
   14 }
   15 
   16 #[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]
   17 pub enum Commands {
   18     /// Push one or more recipes in parallel (all if none named).
   19     Push {
   20         /// Recipe names to execute. Pushes all if omitted.
   21         #[arg(name = "RECIPE")]
   22         recipes: Vec<String>,
   23 
   24         /// Push only the recipes grouped by the named menu (repeatable). Combines
   25         /// with any RECIPE arguments. See the [menus] config section.
   26         #[arg(long = "cookbook", visible_alias = "menu", value_name = "MENU")]
   27         cookbooks: Vec<String>,
   28 
   29         /// Set an environment variable (KEY=VALUE). Overrides CI-derived vars.
   30         #[arg(short = 'e', long = "env", value_name = "KEY=VALUE")]
   31         env: Vec<String>,
   32 
   33         /// Show what would happen without actually running anything.
   34         #[arg(long)]
   35         dry_run: bool,
   36 
   37         /// Disable the TUI and print progress to stdout.
   38         #[arg(long)]
   39         no_tui: bool,
   40 
   41         /// Derive CIABATTA_BRANCH/_COMMIT/_TAG/_BUILD_NUMBER from local git
   42         /// history instead of the configured CI system.
   43         #[arg(long)]
   44         local: bool,
   45 
   46         /// Path to ciabatta.toml (overrides .ciabatta/ciabatta.toml discovery).
   47         #[arg(short = 'c', long)]
   48         config: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
   49     },
   50 
   51     /// Pull (download) artifacts for one or more recipes.
   52     Pull {
   53         #[arg(name = "RECIPE")]
   54         recipes: Vec<String>,
   55 
   56         /// Pull only the recipes grouped by the named menu (repeatable). Combines
   57         /// with any RECIPE arguments. See the [menus] config section.
   58         #[arg(long = "cookbook", visible_alias = "menu", value_name = "MENU")]
   59         cookbooks: Vec<String>,
   60 
   61         #[arg(short = 'e', long = "env", value_name = "KEY=VALUE")]
   62         env: Vec<String>,
   63 
   64         #[arg(long)]
   65         dry_run: bool,
   66 
   67         #[arg(long)]
   68         no_tui: bool,
   69 
   70         /// Derive CIABATTA_BRANCH/_COMMIT/_TAG/_BUILD_NUMBER from local git
   71         /// history instead of the configured CI system.
   72         #[arg(long)]
   73         local: bool,
   74 
   75         #[arg(short = 'c', long)]
   76         config: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
   77     },
   78 
   79     /// Run a deploy recipe: a DAG of dependent script steps with error-recovery
   80     /// branches. Add --gui for a live web view, or --build to design a flowchart.

I have a solid picture now. Here's an overview of the codebase.

## Ciabatta — overview

A cross-platform **CLI for publishing/pulling build artifacts** to registries (Nexus, S3, Artifactory, Docker, ECR), driven by a single `.ciabatta/ciabatta.toml`. Written in **Rust (edition 2024)**, async on Tokio, with a `ratatui` TUI and several embedded web UIs. `Cargo.toml:1-33`, `README.md:7-14`.

### Entry point
`src/main.rs` parses the CLI (`clap`) and dispatches each subcommand. It handles project discovery (walking up to find `.ciabatta/`), environment-variable resolution (git/CI/`-e` merge with a precedence order), and the `CIABATTA_PATH` derivation. `src/main.rs:36-187`, `:384-524`.

### Core artifact flow (the original purpose)
- **`src/config.rs`** — parses the TOML config: registries + recipes + menus.
- **`src/registry/`** — one module per backend: `nexus.rs`, `s3.rs`, `artifactory.rs`, `docker.rs`, `ecr.rs`, plus `browse.rs` (remote exploration) and `mod.rs`.
- **`src/runner.rs`** — drives each recipe through the four-stage state machine (login → pre → main → post) for push/pull/deploy. `README.md:281-296`.
- **`src/ci.rs`** — resolves CI metadata (GitHub, GitLab, Jenkins, CircleCI, Azure, Bitbucket).
- **`src/git.rs`** / **`src/environment.rs`** — local git var sourcing and local-vs-CI mode.
- **`src/tui/`** — the interactive `ratatui` browser (`ciabatta tui`).

### Deploys
`src/deploy/` runs a **DAG of dependent script steps** with error-recovery branches. `engine.rs` executes the graph, `server.rs` serves the live `--gui` view and the `--build` flowchart editor (`index.html`, `builder.html`). `README.md:321-429`, `src/main.rs:69-106`.

### Analyze
`src/analyze/` builds a repo dependency graph (scans `Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `requirements.txt`, Dockerfiles, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, shell scripts) and serves an interactive view. `scan.rs`, `requirements.rs`, `cache.rs`, `server.rs`, `index.html`. `README.md:451-495`.

### Newer / auxiliary features
- **`src/ai/`** — the largest recent addition: an AI assistant with a chat TUI + live mind-map server, providers (Claude/OpenAI/vLLM), tools, sessions, compaction, jobs, "ship" and "burn-in" flows. `src/main.rs:224-288`.
- **`src/todo/`** — a task store with a web app (`ciabatta todo`, can run detached). `src/main.rs:196-213`.
- **`src/watch/`** — runs a command and streams logs into a searchable live web view with bookmarks/triggers. `src/main.rs:293-312`.
- **`src/configure.rs`** — interactive registry setup + `configure auto`.

### Frontend
The `frontend/` dir (Vite + yarn/turbo) is the **project's marketing/docs site** — separate from the embedded HTML UIs that ship inside the binary (each feature module has its own `index.html`).

Want me to go deeper on any one area — e.g. the registry backends, the deploy DAG engine, or the AI assistant?
